points: Temperature is a reliable measure of hotness/coldness, replacing subjective senses like touch.
Clinical thermometers (Digital/Infrared) measure human body temperature. Mercury is toxic and being replaced.
Normal body temperature for a healthy person is ~37.0°C or 98.6°F (variations exist by age/time).
Laboratory thermometers measure wider ranges (-10°C to 110°C) and must be read while immersed in the substance.
The three main scales are Celsius (°C), Fahrenheit (°F), and Kelvin (K). Kelvin is the SI unit.
Conversion formula: Temperature in Kelvin = Temperature in Celsius + 273.15.
Boiling point of water is 100°C; freezing/melting point of ice is 0°C. These stay constant during phase changes.
Absolute Zero is the lowest theoretical temperature (-273.15°C or 0 K).
Anna Mani ('Weather Woman of India') was a leading Indian scientist in meteorological measurements.
